categories.title: Communities & Servers
categories.description: Where Gen Z actually discovers opportunities

items.name: Disboard
items.tip: Search '[your stack] india' or 'internships' — join 2–3 active servers only.

items.name: GitHub Explore
items.tip: Filter good-first-issue repos from startups — PRs beat cold applications.

items.name: Indie Hackers
items.tip: Comment on build logs; founders hire from engaged builders.

items.name: Devfolio
items.tip: Hackathon teams often become internship referrals.

items.name: freeCodeCamp
items.tip: Ship certifications as projects on GitHub, not just badges.

items.name: Frontend Mentor
items.tip: Turn challenges into portfolio case studies with before/after.

items.name: Kaggle Learn
items.tip: Micro-courses + notebooks = proof for AI roles.

items.name: The Odin Project
items.tip: Project-heavy — ideal for students who learn by building.

items.name: Wellfound (AngelList)
items.tip: Startup internships appear here before big portals.

items.name: YC Work at a Startup
items.tip: Short applications — tailor with product-specific hooks.

items.name: LinkedIn
items.tip: Use for DMs & content, not only Easy Apply spam.

items.name: X (Twitter)
items.tip: Follow founders + '#buildinginpublic' for early role signals.

items.name: IDE Bootcamp
items.tip: Best for beginners to start their journey in Hackathons.

items.name: IIT's Hackathon
items.tip: Win or not — submission URL is outreach proof.
